purpose: Gate 2 source-reality contract for the Philippines Building Code Evidence Auditor V2. It defines the four earthquake-only evidence lanes before any target-specific evidence search begins.
global_safety_rule: Missing public evidence must produce no public evidence found or no public answer found. It must never be converted into safe, unsafe, compliant, noncompliant, cleared, no tag, no permit, or not reviewed.

scope_reality.label: NSCP / seismic design evidence
scope_reality.source_reality: Usually not publicly searchable for a named private building. Public sources may show process requirements or rare target-specific engineering references; signed/sealed design documents normally require owner, OBO, or professional disclosure.
accepted_evidence_types: target-specific signed and sealed structural plans naming NSCP or seismic design basis
target-specific structural calculations or design computation records naming seismic analysis
official OBO or Building Official record saying civil or structural plans were reviewed for the target
professional structural engineer report tied to the exact building and date
court, procurement, or public-agency record attaching or quoting the target-specific seismic design evidence

likely_public_sources.name: LGU OBO or Building Official process pages
likely_public_sources.use: Shows process and custodian route, not named-building NSCP evidence by itself.

likely_public_sources.name: DPWH National Building Code references
likely_public_sources.use: Provides legal context only; it does not prove a named building was designed or reviewed for NSCP seismic requirements.

scope_reality.no_evidence_semantics: A complete public search with no target-specific finding means no public evidence found for NSCP/seismic design evidence. It does not mean the building lacks NSCP design, lacks review, is unsafe, or is noncompliant.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim the building is earthquake safe or unsafe.
Do not claim the building is NSCP compliant or noncompliant.
Do not claim no seismic design exists because no public record was found.
Do not treat generic standards pages as target-specific evidence.
manual_request_targets: LGU OBO or Building Official records unit
owner or operator engineering office
civil or structural engineer of record
developer or project manager
court or procurement custodian if the target is publicly owned or litigated
ecc_review_notes: Gate 2 may only lock this lane; evidence collection belongs to Gate 3.
The output must preserve no-public-evidence semantics without making safety or compliance conclusions.

scope_reality.label: OBO structural permit or review evidence
scope_reality.source_reality: Some LGUs publish process pages, citizen charters, or applicant portals, but target-specific structural review records are usually offline, applicant-facing, or released only through manual request.
accepted_evidence_types: target-specific building permit record with civil or structural review details
OBO evaluation, correction, deficiency, notice of action, or plan-review document tied to the target
official inspection report referencing structural permit or review status
public portal result using a valid target-specific application or permit identifier
official certification or certified copy confirming structural review or related permit records

likely_public_sources.name: Quezon City Department of the Building Official
likely_public_sources.use: Public process and custodian context; not a named-building structural review registry.

likely_public_sources.name: Davao City Online Building Permit System application status
likely_public_sources.use: Applicant-facing status route that may require application identifiers before target-specific checking.

scope_reality.no_evidence_semantics: A complete public search with no target-specific finding means no public evidence found for OBO structural permit or review evidence. It does not mean no permit exists, no review happened, or the building is unsafe or noncompliant.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim no OBO review exists because no public web result was found.
Do not claim the building has or lacks a structural permit unless a target-specific source says so.
Do not convert an OBO process page into target-specific review evidence.
Do not infer safety or code compliance from an application portal route.
manual_request_targets: LGU OBO or Building Official
city engineer or structural plan review unit
one-stop shop or building permit application desk
owner, applicant, or authorized representative
BFP only when the structural review question also references occupancy or fire-safety clearance documents
ecc_review_notes: Gate 2 only locks the structural-review evidence question.
A public process route is a manual request target, not a positive target-specific finding.

scope_reality.label: Latest post-earthquake tag / status
scope_reality.source_reality: Official LGU, OBO, OCBO, City Engineer, CDRRMO, DPWH, or BFP sources may publish tag lists or status notices after major earthquakes. News can be useful as a lead but must be separated from official status evidence.
accepted_evidence_types: official green, yellow, or red tag notice tied to the target
official inspected, restricted-use, unsafe, not-safe-for-occupancy, or similar post-earthquake status
official assessment report or public list naming the target and tag or occupancy restriction
official notice requiring repair, comprehensive assessment, or clearance before use
reputable news that quotes or links an official tag/status source, recorded as weaker evidence unless confirmed

likely_public_sources.name: Davao City red and yellow tag announcement
likely_public_sources.use: Official example for post-earthquake red/yellow tag language and building-status handling.

likely_public_sources.name: Davao OCBO tagged buildings safety requirements
likely_public_sources.use: Official example for tag effects, repair methodology, and clearance-before-use context.

scope_reality.no_evidence_semantics: A complete public search with no target-specific finding means no public answer found for latest post-earthquake tag/status. It does not mean no tag exists, the target is cleared, or the target is safe.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim no tag exists because no public answer was found.
Do not treat a news report as official tag status unless it cites or quotes an official source.
Do not treat an old tag/status as current without date and scope caveats.
Do not convert a red, yellow, or green tag into a permanent safety conclusion.
manual_request_targets: LGU OBO, OCBO, or Building Official
City Engineer or municipal engineer
CDRRMO or incident command records custodian
DPWH district or regional office when involved in assessments
BFP when occupancy or closure status is linked to fire-safety inspection records
ecc_review_notes: This is a status-answer lane: no public result means no public answer found.
Gate 3 must preserve the latest date, issuing body, tag color/status, affected portion, and any unresolved exceptions.

scope_reality.label: Latest clearance after damage or tag
scope_reality.source_reality: Clearance after damage or a tag is often less publicly visible than the initial incident. Strong evidence usually requires an official lifting notice, reoccupancy clearance, updated tag, accepted repair completion, or signed/sealed stability certificate tied to the post-earthquake context.
accepted_evidence_types: official reoccupancy clearance tied to the target and post-earthquake event
official lifting notice or updated green/inspected status after a prior red/yellow tag
official acceptance of repair, retrofit, or methodology completion allowing use
signed/sealed structural stability or soundness certificate tied to the damage/tag context
official LGU/OBO/OCBO notice naming the target and stating clearance conditions

likely_public_sources.name: Davao OCBO tagged buildings compliance requirements
likely_public_sources.use: Official context for comprehensive structural assessment, repair methodology, repair permits, and OCBO clearance before use.

likely_public_sources.name: Davao City structural assessments continue
likely_public_sources.use: Official context for assessment workflow and damage-report routing.

scope_reality.no_evidence_semantics: A complete public search with no target-specific finding means no public answer found for latest clearance after damage or tag. It does not mean the target is cleared, uncleared, safe, unsafe, open, or closed.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim the target is cleared unless a target-specific official or professional source says so.
Do not treat reopening, hours, bookings, or operator statements as official clearance.
Do not treat a repair permit or repair plan as final clearance unless acceptance or reoccupancy is explicit.
Do not convert missing public clearance into unsafe, safe, open, or closed.
manual_request_targets: LGU OBO, OCBO, or Building Official
City Engineer or municipal engineer
owner or operator engineering office
civil or structural engineer who issued a stability certificate
CDRRMO, BFP, or DPWH when named in the incident or assessment chain
ecc_review_notes: This is a status-answer lane: no public result means no public answer found.
Gate 3 must separate official clearance from operator claims and preserve unresolved exceptions.

ecc_review.objective: Keep V2 Gate 2 limited to the four earthquake evidence searches and prevent evidence collection before selected lanes are confirmed.
inputs: confirmed exact building identity from Gate 1
default-all or user-selected earthquake evidence lanes
optional earthquake date, location, tag color, permit number, owner, or sublocation clues
outputs: one or more selected earthquake scope IDs
Gate 3 readiness without any evidence search results
eval: selected lanes are one or more of the four required IDs
unsupported non-earthquake requests do not proceed
Gate 2 packets do not include search results or claims
human_boundary: user accepts all four lanes or narrows to one or more lanes
manual custodians are identified only after lanes are selected
legal-grade records require user-authorized manual request outside public search
safety_risks: missing public evidence misread as no permit, no tag, or clearance
official and operator sources mixed together
Gate 2 starting evidence search before scope lock
generic standards treated as target-specific proof
ecc_review.handoff: Gate 3 receives exactly one locked earthquake lane and must build an evidence packet rather than expanding the scope.
ecc_review.overclaim_boundary: The auditor may report public evidence and public gaps. It must not certify safety, compliance, structural adequacy, current occupancy fitness, permit absence, tag absence, or clearance without direct target-specific evidence.
